patterns: Dominant segmented search bar above fold
One saturated action color, grayscale elsewhere
Small labels over short values for multi-part choices
Text-only navigation with light utility controls

designPrinciples.decision: make a segmented care-search bar the dominant control
designPrinciples.reason: users understand the core Dog HQ job immediately
designPrinciples.evidence: Airbnb search is about 850px wide, 66px tall, and centered in the header
designPrinciples.tradeOff: less room for dashboard-heavy hero content

designPrinciples.principle: Use grayscale until action needs color
designPrinciples.decision: use warm red only for primary action and urgent care signals
designPrinciples.evidence: Airbnb uses red primarily on the search CTA

ramzLens.evidence: segmented search maps to the user's service/date/dog/location decision
ramzLens.risk: a pretty dashboard may not answer the first task
ramzLens.change: lead with care search and move operational logs below

ramzLens.evidence: Airbnb-style cards can place ratings, distance, and constraints next to provider names
ramzLens.risk: generic trust claims are less useful than visible details
ramzLens.change: show concrete provider and care signals

ramzLens.evidence: Airbnb limits saturated color and above-fold noise
ramzLens.risk: too many chips/status colors make Dog HQ feel like admin software
ramzLens.change: use one action color and one trust signal per card

antiPatterns: decorative pet imagery replacing product tasks
implementationNotes: Keep editable Dog HQ utilities in light cards
